utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey lawrence . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hey , good to see you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it's good to see you too . so , i know the nurse told you about dax .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'd like to tell dax a little bit about you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , lawrence is a 62-year-old male , with a past medical history significant for type i diabetes , congestive heart failure , depression , and reflux , who presents with complaints of shortness of breath . so lawrence , what's been going on ? wh- what's wrong with your breathing ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , i , i've noticed that i've been swelling up a little bit . i think a lot of it has to do with going to some house parties , eating some salty foods . i feel really lethargic .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and when you get short of breath , are you short of breath when you're just sitting here ? do you feel short of breath when you're walking ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's something like walking up a flight of stairs i'll actually feel it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . now , um , how long has this been going on for ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: probably about 10 days .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and you said you're noticed some swelling in your legs ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: a little bit .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . do you have problems lying flat in bed ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , i'm a little uncomfortable when i wake up in the morning and i feel pretty stiff . and , and , like , it takes me a little while to adjust to walking when i get up .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . do you ever wake up acutely short of breath at night ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , it happened once , probably about a week ago , but it has n't happened since . so i have n't been that concerned about it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . in terms of your , your diabetes , how are you doing with that ? i know you're on the insulin pump .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , are your blood sugars okay ? have you noticed a spike in them recently ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , i have n't . i've been pretty good about monitoring it so i ... during the working week , i stay on top of that . but on saturdays or sundays we're so busy i forget to monitor it . but i have n't seen anything spike .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and i remember your affinity for chocolate bars , so ...

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: guilty as charged . i love chocolate bars .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . so , in terms of your depression , how are you doing with that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , it's been a crazy year and a half . um , i've tried some natural solutions . in the summer i did stretches outdoors every morning , which was great , but now the weather changed and , uh , you know , i've got ta find some alternatives . i'm not ready to try any medications just yet , so-

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: . i'm open to some suggestions .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . we'll talk about that .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and then in terms of your reflux , how are you doing ? i know we had you on the omeprazole .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you were making some lifestyle modifications , cutting back on your red bull and caffeine .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alcohol intake has been ... how's that been ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: tough during the holiday season , but i'm , i'm better .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and no issues with your depression ? you do n't want to hurt yourself or anyone else ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no . no . absolutely not .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , all right . well , let's go ahead . i know you did a review of system sheet when you checked in and you endorsed the shortness of breath .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: any other symptoms ? chest pain , fever , chills , cough ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , belly pain ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . let's go ahead . i want to move on to a physical exam .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon ? show me the vital signs . so here in the office , you know , your vital signs look good . your , your pulse ox ... that's your oxygenation level ... looks good . so i'm encouraged by that with you just sitting here , okay ? i'm gon na go ahead and do another , uh , just check you over . i'll let you know what i find .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . so , on your exam things look okay . so , i do n't appreciate any jugular venous distension or any carotid bruits on your neck exam . on your heart exam , i do appreciate a slight 3 out of 6 systolic ejection murmur . on your lung exam , i do appreciate some crackles , bilaterally , at the bases . and on your lower extremity exam i do appreciate 1+ pitting edema . so what does all that mean ? so , i do think that you're retaining some fluid . it's probably from some of the dietary indiscretion .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: but i wan na go ahead and look at some of the results , okay ? i had the nurse do a chest x-ray on you before we came in , okay ? hey , dragon ? show me the test x-ray . so i reviewed the results of your chest x-ray and it looks good . there's no e- evidence of any airspace disease , but that does n't mean that you still ca n't be retaining some fluid , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon ? show me the labs . and your labs look okay . you know , you do n't have an elevated white blood cell count so i'm not really concerned about infection . we saw the chest x-ray , there's no pneumonia , so that's good .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , let's talk a little bit about , you know , my assessment and my plan for you . okay ? so , for your first problem of your shortness of breath i think that you are in an acute heart failure exacerbation . i want to go ahead and , uh , put you on some lasix , 40 milligrams a day . i want you to weigh yourself every day .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: if your weight is n't going down , uh , or if it's going up i want you to call me . certainly , if you get more short of breath at rest i want you to go ahead and call me or call 911-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . and go into the emergency room . you might need some intravenous diuretics .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay ? for your second problem of your type i diabetes , um , let's go ahead ... i wan na order a hemoglobin a1c for , um , uh , just in a , like a month or so , just to see if we have to make any adjustments . and i want you to follow up with your endocrinologist . okay ? and i also want to make sure that you have a recent eye exam . all right ? uh , for your third problem of your depression , let's go ahead and refer you to psychiatry , just for some , um , like , talk therapy to help you through that . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no medications . and for your fourth problem of your reflux , let's go ahead and continue you on the omeprazole , 20 milligrams a day . do you have any questions , lawrence ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not at this point .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . um , uh , again , i want you to call me if you have any problems with your breathing , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you got it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon ? finalize the notes ...

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 62-year-old male with a history of type 1 diabetes, congestive heart failure, depression, and reflux.
Shortness of breath
The patient reports leg swelling and lethargy, attributing symptoms to recent dietary intake of salty foods.
The doctor asks if the patient experiences shortness of breath at rest or with exertion.
The patient experiences shortness of breath when walking up stairs.
The doctor asks about the duration of the shortness of breath.
The shortness of breath has been present for approximately 10 days.
The doctor asks if the patient has difficulty lying flat in bed.
The patient reports discomfort lying flat, morning stiffness, and difficulty adjusting to walking upon waking.
The doctor asks if the patient wakes up acutely short of breath at night.
The patient experienced nocturnal shortness of breath once, about a week ago.
Diabetes
The doctor asks if the patient's blood sugars are stable.
The patient monitors blood sugar during the work week but is inconsistent on weekends; he reports no recent spikes.
Depression
The patient manages depression with outdoor stretching but needs alternatives due to seasonal changes; he declines medication.
Reflux
The doctor asks about the patient's alcohol intake.
The patient reports difficulty reducing alcohol intake during the holidays but is improving.
The doctor asks if the patient has suicidal or homicidal ideation.
The patient denies suicidal and homicidal ideation.
The doctor asks about chest pain, fever, chills, and cough.
The patient denies chest pain, fever, chills, and cough.
The doctor asks about abdominal pain.
The patient denies abdominal pain.
The patient's vital signs and pulse oximetry are normal.

Physical exam reveals no JVD or carotid bruits, a 3/6 systolic ejection murmur, bilateral crackles at lung bases, and 1+ pitting edema; the doctor suspects fluid retention due to dietary indiscretion.
Chest x-ray shows no evidence of airspace disease.
Labs are within normal limits, including WBC count.
Initiate Lasix 40mg daily.
Patient to weigh himself daily and contact the doctor if weight increases or if dyspnea at rest occurs.
Order hemoglobin A1c in one month, follow up with endocrinologist, and obtain an eye exam.
Refer to psychiatry for talk therapy.
Continue omeprazole 20mg daily.
The doctor asks if the patient has any questions.
The patient has no further questions.

case_description: shortness of breath; type 1 diabetes;congestive heart failure;depression;reflux
